assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states 56 nodes can upgrade in parallel (8 pools × 7 nodes) and doesn't mention GKE's ~20 node parallelism limit

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: Response calculates based on incorrect parallelism assumptions and doesn't provide the ~150 batch calculation based on 20-node limit

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends staggered multi-window approach with Week 1 for GPU pools and Week 2 for CPU pools

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Response suggests upgrading GPU pools first, stating they are 'higher priority,' which contradicts the expectation of prioritizing CPU pools as less disruptive

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention scheduling GPU pool upgrades during gaps between training/inference workloads

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Response suggests using multiple windows and mentions the approach 'likely requires 2-3 maintenance windows'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how it interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention GKE node limits, either the 15,000 standard limit or the 65,000 limit for AI workloads in GKE 1.31+
